title: ruscus aculeatus var. laxus
intro: Ruscus aculeatus var. laxus, also known as Butcher's Broom, is a type of flowering plant in the Asparagaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, astringent, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Butcher's Broom include improving circulation, reducing swelling, and relieving symptoms of varicose veins and hemorrhoids. Butcher's Broom can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, and is often used to treat various health conditions due to its potential to improve blood flow and alleviate pain.
